text:
Just cancelled service with T-Mobile last month. They raised the final bill $150, I spent 3-4 hours speaking with customer service and still ended up with a non-answer. I finally just paid them off to be rid of them.

When we signed up in their store, the employee added a 6th line to my 5 member family, and when I got that removed they took a bunch of our deals with it. Don't believe a word they say in the store. I sure hope that employee's commission was worth the hundreds of dollars they screwed me out of.

I stayed with T-Mobile for years because the service was decent and the prices were fair. But the way they treated me when I left was disappointing. After switching providers, I received a $230 final bill — no detailed breakdown, no goodwill gesture, no attempt to keep the relationship respectful.

Their slogan reads: “Get Even More Without Paying More.” Unfortunately, my experience says otherwise. You will pay more, especially on the way out.

T-Mobile, you had a customer who might’ve returned. Now you’ve got a review. If someone from your marketing or retention team still values loyalty, I’m open to a real conversation.

Tmobile is fraudulent. It does not deserve a 1 star rating. I joined them because I was told I would pay $80 for my business lines. I never knew they lied to me just to sell to me. They sent me a bill of $180 in a month, then I decided to port my lines to a different carrier in February. I was told I needed to pay an outstanding bill of $122.89 before I can port my numbers, I paid and in April I got another bill for $134.66. I asked to know why, they told me because I never completed the porting of my lines which was weird because non of my lines was with them. I paid the $134.66 regardless. Then in May, I received another bill of $7.77, wondering what this is for. Run away from them. I will also be seeking legal action against this fraudulent act.

I went in to t-mobile Alameda and Wadsworth and I had the best customer experience ever. Mary helped me transfer from Verizon to t-mobile. I got into a 16 pro phone with a cool watch phone and paying less then I did with my old service and she did all the transfers easy and I’m so happy with all my devices. Love T-Mobile and there customer service knocks it out of the park ❤️

on 05-16-25 aprox 5:30 . went to tmobile on rt 44 in new smyrna beach florida. 3 people working there. one was diligently working, other two, Not so much. tall male told me 2 people ahead of me. No problem. waited 10 minutes (mind you only 2 customers in store)  and tall male hols up 1 finger (as in 1 minute) and disappears for 45 minute. Female (red hair) at desk does similar tells me "one minute" and vanishes. other employee still working customers and other 2 gone into back room, seen sitting just hanging out laughing. all i wanted to do was either turn in or contact owner of cell phone I found on road. still working, but i couldnt open due to password. finally last customer walks out and i rush to last employee, he said he was off at 5:00 and was seemingly decent guy. 10 minutes later tall male comes out of back, states "nothing he can do theres no way to find out whos phone it is. I told him he could check the sim card. (hopefully he already knew that...) but didnt want to help or work. he pulls out the sim, then says there wasnt one there, and its NOT a tmobile phone. I ended up waiting for almost an hour to have 2/3 inept, lousy employees jerk me around thinking it funny. I have been with tmo for 10+ years, but am currently shopping for another carrier that has normal employees, NOT lazy, sarcastic, deadbeats "working" there that spend more time trying to avoid work than actually DOING any work. so phone cannot be returned as tmobile on rt44 in New Smyrna took the sim card out and palmed it. I tried to get it back to owner wasted time  and got insulted by tmo on rt 44 NSB. If ity were my store I would fire both of them instead of them blowing customers off. I took a couple pix and videos of me being only person in front of store for 5 minutes. will try to post if able.

Since my husband passed in November I have struggled.  He paid all the bills so to remember and also keep track of month/day is not so easy.  And yes, I thought I had till Saturday but wow it was probably five Saturdays ago! And yes I had my phones turned off.  But the young man in the Festus office and the young woman on the phone, who both were just awesome, got me to the third person, another young man, who helped me get things taken care of so I could move forward again.  My problem, my fault but they were very kind and helped me fix it.  I can not thank each one of them enough.  The company must be good and have a moral code if they have people like those three working there.  Thank you.

This is stuff people don't usually review. I have a Galaxy A15 5G. It came with 150 pre loaded apps, most of which have nothing to do with the functionality of the phone. There are some, Galaxy Store, for instance, that you CANNOT uninstall or turn off notifications for. Disabling is pointless. It comes up by itself, so it doesn't go into deep sleep. Samsung is very obnoxious about this. It is in two places on my home screen. Another thing: If you block a number, You will still get voicemail, because the phone doesn't ring. No way for them to fix it. That's just the way of it. Unless the last person I spoke to about it is right, in which case, he told me to download T-life. I did. It's an app of ads. I'm looking for another carrier. One that isn't a control freak about MY phone and the service *I* am paying for. I'll have to just get rid of the voicemail until I find another carrier that isn't a control freak.
waiting forever
Good coverage in cities, but rural areas still have dead zones. Customer service is friendly, though wait times can be long.

My loyalty, demonstrated by consistent service for over two decades has been met with frustrating and unacceptable customer service.

My primary concern involves my 77-year-old father's broken, paid-off phone, which is still under warranty. His DL expired  (by one month only). The required procedures to address the issue were excessively burdensome and unreasonable. The demanded to add an “authorized user” with no connection to my account solely to initiate service was particularly risky and burdensome. Further compounding the problem was the refusal to transfer the SIM card and media from the damaged phone to its replacement, without taking the old phone indefinitely. This lack of proactive troubleshooting and the insistence on unnecessary steps demonstrate a concerning lack of customer empathy, along with the willingness to compromise an ACCOUNT HOLDER OF 23 yrs! 

Beyond this specific incident, my numerous interactions with T-Mobile representatives have been characterized by repetitive scripted responses, an overwhelming emphasis on upselling, and an overall disregard for the time and inconvenience imposed upon loyal, long-term customer.

In order to get my elderly father assistance in another state I was forced to add another “authorized user.”Their priority is revenue generation over customer satisfaction. Every department should not be attempting to upsell! The adherence to policy over practical problem-solving, along with their resistance to address legitimate issues efficiently, are beyond frustrating. I intend to discontinue my service with T-Mobile and purchase my phones outright from another provider to avoid any future issues. When is the FCC going to do something about all of these complaints, or are 6000 people lying. Wells Fargo was held accountable for similar allegations why is T-Mobile exempt of accountability????

T-Mobile has failed me as a customer and a fraud victim. Over $4,000 has been stolen from me due to negligence at Store #9523 in Houston, TX. A rep falsely marked my iPhone 14 Pro as returned, giving my ex unauthorized access to my account. I’ve reported this over 100 times—via calls, certified letters, FCC, FTC, BBB, and my bank—but T-Mobile has done nothing. I’ve been ignored, gaslit, and billed for devices I didn’t authorize. No real fraud department, no accountability, no resolution.

I just can't rap my head around I tried explaining that my payment went to Metro instead of T-Mobile but this is my problem I signed up with T-Mobile about 3 months ago and to surprise my payments were going to Metro but why wouldn't T-Mobile reach out to see why I'm not paying but I was they let my internet get 3 months behind and cut me off and I accept if no payment please disconnect my service but I'm one who pays her bills I like living comfortable I don't play games single African American women that's responsible and pay my bills but my internet is off with no answer about getting payment back from Metro I just thought T-Mobile was a little more understanding then that.

Never purchase a phone from Metro PCS now known as T-Mobile... What they didn't tell me is that the phone is locked for 365 days of service. Although you pay month to month and your phone is paid in full, you can't use your paid phone as you please to go else where like a company with better customer service and prices. I've been with Metro PCS aka T-Mobile for 22 years. I bought a new phone on my birthday because I had a cracked screen and the phone was outdated, then 30 days later I've found a company with better customer service and prices only to find out that I'm locked in Metro T Mobile because of the new phone purchase although I've been with Metro PCS for 22 years. The staff at the  Store clerks were also very childish and unprofessional. Long story short, DON'T BUY A PHONE from Metro because of hidden terms and conditions. They won't tell you about the phone is locked for 365 days for Metro PCS/ T Mobile service only. If they were honest and upfront with this information I would not complain after the purchase. WOW... 86 percent of 1 Star complaints is very telling!

My phone is not working wont charge. I tried going online to see about getting a new phone, but I cannot because they send you a text to verify your identity, but phone is not working. I call on the phone and they ask me for a 6-digit pin that I made 6 years ago and i do not remember it. Because I do not remember this pin from 6 years ago, they cannot help me over the phone. I work full time go to school full time and I'm a full-time mom so if you are a busy person T-Mobile is not the place for your business because they will not help you over the phone, they will require you to go to a store hour away. After being a customer for 25 years before it was even called t mobile, I will be canceling my plan. I will also be sure to let ever single person I know how horrible the customer service is at T-Mobile. I will spread it far and wide. TMOLIBE SUCKS

Hands down worst cell company in history. They suck at everything. Customer service sucks, cellphones suck, the service is the worst! Atnt is a close 2nd but tmobile has em beat. Tmobile is the worst. Dint take my word, google it,says tmobile is the worst company in this country at everything. I made mistake of switching to these morons and I should have known when something is too good to be true...they gave us free cellphones and only required 1 cell to trade in. I should have known it's because service sucks, calls drop daily, you can never get connected when at home or driving anywhere. I called to complain but they only hire morons too, that never do a thing to help you. I started taking screen shot everytime I saw issue like text not going through or connection lost or internet down but after 11 months I have 100 pics, that's how often there is a problem, it's everyday! I am definitely leaving tmobile as soon as I can. I post everywhere warning others to stay away from this incompetent cell carrier. Worst in the business! In fact they don't get 1 thing right. I woukd post it if they did,but I have nothing positive to say. They even offer free junk when I call to complain and still im saying stay away. How bad must they suck for someone to advise stay away even when free.

edits.text: Tmobile worst cell carrier on the market. Don't be fooled by people that leave excellent ratings. I can assure you it's either bs or they never had a cell issue, which is just luck. I've heard people say tmobile sucks, but I tried it anyway. I should have listened. Everything that can go wrong, they do! And this is with new service and a new phone. What's worse their customer service is non existent ! Absolute worst, do not waste your time and money, believe the rumors, they really do suck!

text:
-I tried switching to T-Mobile in 09/2024
-they approved me and collected $445.29 in tax and down payment for 3x iPhones and one internet device on a brand new 5 line, one internet account and ship me all the devices to my mothers house since she is retired and always home 
-the day after I receive the equipment in hand, they cancel my account due to fraud (I believe this is because my billing address and shipping address for the equipment were different)
-after 2 months of trying to verify that I am not doing any fraud, I give up and return via return label provided by t mobile. All the equipment was factory sealed still. I never opened anything.  
-in December 2024 I received a confirmation email that they received the equipment and I should be receiving a refund soon
-today is May 12, 2025 and I still have not received my refund for the taxes and downpayment til this day. on May 8th 2025 I chatted with a T-Mobile business rep thru Facebook messenger, again confirmed they received all my equipment (I sent them all the IMEIs that I received and the return of the exact IMEIs was confirmed) but still refuse to refund me in any way. Even to refund the same credit card that was used to purchase the equipment with to begin with

They are basically the ones committing the fraud here. They are scamming me and stole my $445.29. I did not commit any fraud in anyway whatsoever. I created an account using a business that I am the 100% owner of and returned the equipment after they deemed my documents not sufficient enough to verify. Someone tell me what fraud I committed please? I am the victim of fraud here by a huge company called T-Mobile who collected $445.29 in taxes and downpayment and kept it after I returned my equipment. Please help me get my money back from this fraudulent company. They are thieves and scammers. Any human with a brain cell can look at this situation and see they stole my money. They should be in jail

I have been with T-Mobile for 10 years. Cellphone service for the most part has been good. Customer  service on the other hand is terrible. None of the staff working at an actual store know what they are doing and do not take accountability for anything. They just tell you to call Customer service as they can't fix any account issues at the store. So, then a person is stuck either calling or texting Customer service and wasting half the day so that in the end, absolutely nothing gets resolved because none of them know WTF they are doing. 
Recently, my husband and I went to our local store simply to buy him a new phone. The male associate told us how we were currently paying too much for our plan and that we should switch to this different plan and there was a promotion going on  so that my husband would get his phone for half price and we would get an IPad and a Samsung tablet for free. Yes, he said BOTH. All we had to do was pay the taxes. We get home and discover the p360 insurance we had on 2 of our phones never got put back onto the plan and no insurance was put on the new phone, Ipad, or Tablet. This could've been a real problem if something would've happened to one of our devices. So, I went in my account and opened a chat with a "specialist" to get the insurance put on. What a sh*t show that was. Turns out that 2 hours later, the insurance was put on,  but was being charged the wrong price. Then, I get my monthly bill. For years, my bill was always $280 a month. This new plan, the was supposed to save me money, has now produced my monthly bill to be $410. I took the printouts of all my bills to my local T-Mobile store to hopefully get answers and the issue  resolved. The associate informed me that I hadn't gotten credits for any of the promotions and that I was being too much for the insurance. Then, he ----- the same associate who talked us into this plan and said BOTH the IPad and Tablet were free, now tells us that only the Tablet was free and the Ipad was half off. Also, the promotion for the IPhone was for half off. So, we paid for half of the phone, therefore the phone should've been all paid for. But, the associate tells us that no, only some of that went towards the phone and the rest went towards the IPhone and Tablet.......which were supposed to be free. I was told it would cost $30 for the 2 data lines for those devices, yet they billed me $53. Nothing I was told that day was correct or legit. The associate either truly wasn't trained in on his position correctly, or he just didn't care and scammed us into purchasing and paying for more than we needed. Then when I said that I just wanted to return the items and go back to my old plan, I was told I would have to pay $120 in restocking fees for the Ipad and Tablet. The store associate told me that he contacted his manager about issuing me a $100 bill credit and that he would call me the following day to confirm. That phone call was never received. The Specialist we spoke to over the phone while at the store said he was going to escalate these issues up and call me on that Monday to see if they could do anything to resolve the issues. That call was never received either. So, it turns out that basically every T-Mobile employee I spoke with that day lied straight to my face and fed me a big spoonful of bullsh*t. I feel swindled, scammed, cheated, and lied to and that is completely unacceptable. This may be my sign to leave T-Mobile and make the switch to another carrier.

I have been a costumer for years. I left another company because it was shady and I feel t-mobile is playing the same games. I subscribed for a free version of Netflix and they switched it on their own to the paid one. I then unsubscribed and they were still charging me till I call.

To bad they are playing these games. 

Always check you bill.

My experience with TMobile has been nothing but wasted money, time and stress. Since switching from Verizon to them I’ve filed 3 claims for phones that have been consistently inoperable. The most recent event was yesterday where I spent 4 hours on the phone including two hours today, where I’ve been getting the run around. They tried to tell me I was’t paying my insurance premium when it’s automatically taken out of my bill totaling $700/month, so they not only told me I was uncovered but then retracted and said I was. I still don’t have the phone that’s needed to run my business and they tried to offer me $50 to compensate for the inconvenience and time. Their associates are not trained properly and have poor documentation as I’ve repeatedly explained the issue to over 5 representatives. The end result is: no phone! I highly recommend not switching or using T-Mobile. I have not been compensated for my time or money I’ve lost and felt humiliated when they assumed I didn’t pay my insurance payment.

Have had t-mobile for about 10 years. For a short period about 5-6 years ago had a problem the first 5 times I called. The 6th time the problem was taking care of and the gal had a phone overnight to me. But what was supposed to be a brand new phone 4 months ago and have had nothing but problems from the minute I got the phone. Games downloaded and played (brand new phone) screen kept going black while on phone calls couldn't hang up. Last week when they reset my phone it took away my internet and at random kept saying no photos selected. Today it started smoking. Spent 2 hours on the phone with them telling me I had to go to the store. Got to the store the gal looked at my phone 3-4 times then handed it to the guy that was working there he looked started tearing my phone apart put it back together kept flipping my phone around reading all the notes in the computer then dropped my phone on the counter. It cracked the camera so it's no longer a factory warranty now I'm responsible for buying another new phone.

Innovation & Marketing excell at T-mobile. As far as consumer experience, there are no stars. I'm not an expert on ADA compliance. But accessibility of their products may not provide fair platforms for people of disabilities. As I age I find myself marginalized by corporate decisions that serve only the predictable normal consumers.

Needed to sort out a few issues with my fathers T mobile home Internet service in Lancaster Pa 

We first went to the Centreville Road store and were met with crushingly bad service. I wish I had filmed it as the video would have went viral 

We then looked to go to the Belmont store but saw it was closed for renovations so we went to the Park city Mall store

In this store we were greeted by Gamaliel C (I asked for his card). This guy was the internet whisperer. He was like a conductor with a symphony. He went through each issue and sorted each and ever one out while telling us what he was doing to do so 

Everything was completed and dad was up and running with his Internet that hour
We received proof of payment for an earlier payment made and a slightly cheaper monthly rate

I am in sales and always have been. This gent is at the top of his game and I know he will be extremely successful in the future

I do hope he sees this comment as he made my father and myself very happy 

Thank you Gamaliel. 

Regards. Peter

Stood at T-Mobile in Panama City Beach for 45 min without being waited on.  Contacted them online, letting them know AT&T had a $50.00 monthly unlimited plan and Consumer Cellular had a $25.00 monthly unlimited plan with 5mg data (which is not enough but even with unlimited data it would be under $50.00  When advised of this the operator (Ivy) took over five minutes to respond back.  .  Guess they don't need the business.

Had tmobile for 2 years, service was terrible where we lived. After the 2 year contract was up, i switched services mid month. I called tmobile to get my final bill since i didn't have their service a full billing cycle. I was told by their customer service rep I would have to go to local tmobile store for the info. I went to local store and was told i would have to call customer service. I went  home and called and told to wait for final billing. I was trying to avoid late charge. Then i was contacted by a 3rd party collection within 3 weeks and was told i owed for the full month plus late fees.I pleaded my case to them and was pretty much told to  f*** off.  I highy recommend anyone to avoid tmobile. Terrible customer service.

Rude workers, crazy expensive for crap services, phone that’s paid off is still locked and held for ransom by company when asked to switch over, I dealing with my brothers death and paying for expenses making budget tight and they’re not whiling to be flexible for the mouth and cut my phone off the next day, they are RUDE, and DONT care about their consumers, they’re are money hungry individuals, I highly recommend ANY other phone company.

THERE WAS AN UPDATE TO MY SAMSUNG PHONE THAT I DID. AFTER THE UPDATE MY PHONE DID NOT WORK PROPERLY. I CALLED TMOBILE AND SPENT OVER 4HRS ON THE PHONE, AND THEY KEPT DISCONNECTING ME, AND THEN SOME INCOMPETENT PERSON OVERSEAS DISCONNECTED MY SERVICES FOR @ 15 HOURS! I AM DISABLED AND HAVE MAJOR HEALTH ISSUES AND I DID NOT HAVE ANY WAY TO COMMUNICATE WITH ANYONE IN THE EVENT I HAD A MEDICAL EMERGENCY. THEY STILL HAVE NOT EXPLAINTED TO ME WHAT HAPPENED, AND WHAT THEY ARE GOING TO DO TO COMPENSATE ME! I HAVE BEEN A CUSTOMER FOR OVER 26 YEARS!
"Unlimited" data turns to unusable 0.5Mbps after 50GB, bills keep creeping up, and customer service means hours on hold. Switching to Verizon ASAP.
Tj is a great employee , very friendly & make sure he help & leave the customer satisfied I was very pleased
Stay away from t mobile run from t mobile the  t must be for terrible  !!!!!!!!

I was a T-Mobile customer for many years and had a great experience back then, so I recently decided to return. That’s when the nightmare began.

About two weeks ago, I visited a T-Mobile store to sign up for 4 lines at $25 each and take advantage of the “phones on us” offer with eligible trade-ins. The in-store rep said I needed to complete the phone purchases via the T-Life app, but the app failed to process the payments. He then advised me to order through Customer Service.

What followed was two full days of failed attempts. My last call alone lasted 4 hours during work hours. None of my credit cards worked—not due to any bank issue, but because T-Mobile wasn’t sending the transactions through. I confirmed this directly with my banks (two different ones). Eventually, T-Mobile admitted the problem and processed the payment.

But that wasn’t the end. They incorrectly logged only 3 trade-ins, and added the 4th phone to my bill. I called again; the rep acknowledged the mistake and fixed it. Then my first bill arrived—with an extra $40 charge. Another call, another rep, another promise to correct it. I'm still waiting on that fix.

One of the new phones arrived defective. After a two-day investigation, Customer Service agreed to exchange it and told me to visit a store. The first store refused to help. So did the second. At the third, I was grilled about whether it had been more than 14 days (it hadn’t), as they clearly wanted to avoid assisting. They eventually processed the exchange—but charged me taxes for a 5th phone. I was promised a refund, but based on everything so far, I have serious doubts.

This experience has caused me days of stress and even insomnia. Joining the "T-Mobile family" has been nothing short of exhausting.
Cassie is a wonderful representative. Understands customer needs. Keeps her customer satisfied. I'm enjoying my home internet service in my area.

T-Mobile has provided a truly miserable customer service experience. I ordered a new device from them directly for the first time, after 20+ years of being a Sprint/T-Mobile Customer. After 5 days had passed, I contacted customer service and found out the device was on backorder. At no point had this been brought to my attention previously, and I expressed disappointment about that. Customer service then stated that they would ensure that my order shipped within 24 hours. This was just to get me off the line with a positive resolution. When contacted next day, they told me they could not ship my order and that it would instead be several more days, then attempted to minimize the fact that their exact words in writing stated "we will ensure that this device is shipped out within 24 hours". Afterwards, I was prompted to complete a survey in regard to the customer service experience, where a supervisor then emailed me as a follow up. I replied to that email as instructed, with a detailed explanation of the interactions I had with customer service. 4 days have passed with no response. Further attempts to contact customer service have also been futile.

My Experience:
Switched to T-Mobile for their "Unlimited Everything" plan. Worst decision ever.

The Problems:

"5G" That Doesn't Work

Full bars but can't load Google Maps

Speed tests show 0.5Mbps (yes, less than 1)

Their solution: "Try standing near a window"

Bill Surprises

Promised $50/month

First bill: $85 ("Regulatory fees" + "5G access charge")

Random $20 increases every few months

Customer Service Hell

2+ hour wait times

Transferred 6 times just to ask a simple question

Reps reading scripts with no power to help

Security Failures

SIM swapped TWICE (lost $3,500 total)

Their "protection": Asking for my mother's maiden name (public on Facebook)

The Last Straw:
When I complained about slow speeds, they said:
"Unlimited doesn't mean fast"

Ever since I went I wish I haven’t day one my bill was wrong I was promised $150 bill and I was paying $250 for almost a year till a manager at the store was helping out because it was done wrong from the beginning and now he can’t even do that help.. horrible and about to switch.

T-mobile has the worst customer service EVER! I am going back to Verizon. I have been charged multiple charges due to a mistake on this company not registering a returned device. I have spent HOURS calling and chatting with them, only to be told that the person I am talking to can see the problem but cannot fix it.  In the mean time I am put off for weeks while nothing is done and charges now continue to mount on a trade in device they say was never credited.  I never received the new phone and have tracking PROOF was returned as undeliverable to the T-Mobile warehouse. I have proof they received my trade in.  None of this has been recorded or accounted for. I am livid. Top all this off with the fact that they raised my rates on my SIX lines after falsely advertising that my rates "will never go up." I am a business owner and an influential person in my community.  I will not recommend T-Mobile to any person after this experience.  In fact, I would recommend any cell service over T-Mobile at this point.

I’ve been a T-Mobile customer for years and originally switched from AT&T because of T-Mobile’s excellent customer service and clear communication. Unfortunately, that standard has declined significantly.

Recently, I was convinced by multiple T-Mobile reps to upgrade to the Military Next plan under the promise of “free annual upgrades.” I was told I could upgrade my devices each year with no extra monthly cost. That turned out to be completely false.

Only after upgrading—and paying $75 more per month—was I told I’d have to:
	•	Start monthly payments of ~$15 per line (for five lines)
	•	Pay off at least 50% of each device before I could “upgrade”

Had I been told that upfront, I never would have changed plans.

Worse, when I asked to return to my original Military Magenta Max plan, I was told it was grandfathered and no longer available. I ended up moving to a cheaper plan—but lost benefits I previously had, all because of bad information given by T-Mobile staff.

Let me be clear: I do not blame the frontline associates. They were respectful and helpful—especially Allie, who was honest with me. My issue is with T-Mobile’s leadership, who are putting undertrained employees in a position where they can’t provide accurate answers. That’s not a people problem—it’s a company problem.

T-Mobile used to be the gold standard in customer experience. Now I can’t even get reliable answers to basic questions. I’ll be shopping for another carrier, and I’m sharing this experience here because it speaks to a much larger breakdown in trust and transparency.

If it was possible to give 0 stars, I would. 1 star is too high. Do better, T-Mobile.

T-Mobile as a company, cell phone and internet provider was the worst experience I have ever had in over 20 years with these services. Onboarding required 5 separate trips to the retail store, my first few bills were overcharged which was acknowledged by the company but not reimbursed, every month there was issues with the phone and internet service, and terrible customer service. I had no choice but to end my service early and unfortunately the negative theme with this company continued. After I stopped my service with T-Mobile they continued to charge me for 3 months of service AFTER I left, and to finish things off when I called to pay my remaining balance for the equipment charges that I was more than willing to pay, they advised me the balance was written off and I could only deal with the collections company. What?! I am a financially responsible individual with all my credit scores in the 800s and have not had anything go to collections in over 25 years when I was a younger man. The fact that I called to pay my remaining balance off and they already sent it to collections should show people how unethical and predatory this company really is. It’s crazy to me that a company can still get away with something like this. Don’t be conned into their deals, it cost me a lot of money, time, and negatively impacted my credit score.

We switched to T-Mobile and then found out that we do not have good coverage in our town. We cancelled right away and now the bills keep coming from T-Mobile for hundreds of dollars that we do not owe. Please help us resolve this!
Call Larry at 605-680-1790
Thank you!

I was a customer of T-Mobile for almost 14 years and had 8 phone lines plus an IPad and never missed or was late on a payment. I decided to switch because the bills were getting extraordinarily high. I had a outstanding bill. I spoke to T-Mobile and they agreed to allow me to make the payments for six months to pay off the bill cause I bought a bunch of phones I’d eight lines and now they’re charging me an extra $18 per month to pay off the bill and I told them that I can’t afford that I was paying over 400 and dollars a month And they’re gonna continue to charge me the late fees so in my opinion, T-Mobile is not a very good company and I would definitely not recommend them unless they decide to change their policy especially for a customer who’s been a customer for 14 years!
Unlimited data actually means unlimited, and their customer reps are chill. But their rural coverage is still weak. If you’re in the city, it’s a W.

If I could give a negative Star it would be -100! All deception and lies!!!! Lied about 15 minute wait, usually an hour and half. Lied about service, which ends up in S.O.S. or barely 1 bar! Lied about 300 rebate in mail that never came! Lied about giving credit and calling back! Lied about fixing issues and that there would be adequate bars for service!  This company is nothing but lies and switching shell games of B.S.! Not Happy!  Don't trust them!
Warning...if you had a Sprint lease they will charge you in perpetuity if you didn't upgrade your phone. Contact me for legal help

I am so fed up with T-Mobile's internet dropping off continuously! You try to call in for support and all you get is there's a wait or someone who doesn't know what the heck they are doing! I am done with their internet service. I ordered another service today due to the continual frustration of trying to work and I can't. Cell phone service is great but T-Mobile needs to get out of the internet service racket. It's pretty crappy!

Never have my screen protectors @ ANY locations, if it's not apple forget it. 

Customer service is always awful . Was assaulted by an employee his name is Kyle and he works at Fall River location . Asked T-Mobile for video footage... They said get a subpoena 🤣 what a joke of a company. Also hide fees in all you're stuff. Been with them over 10 years and that means squat in there eyes...

Still don't have my screen protector either why do I have to ship to house in order to get it. 

Store in Middletown was supposed to have ordered them and call me .. month later they never got them . 😂

Could go on about more issues. Dropped calls . Texts not going through . Always issues with 5 g or any g. 

Just another un american company digging their teeth in honest citizens..

Also if they could ACTUALLY STOP SHARING MY DATA   THAT WOULD BE 👍

I’ve been a long-time customer of various wireless providers, but my recent experience with T-Mobile has been hands down the most frustrating and dishonest interaction I've had with any company.

It started when we were promised a specific set of phones at a set price—only to later find out that neither the phones nor the pricing were actually available. No one from the store contacted us about the issue; instead, I found out through an impersonal email that our order had been cancelled by the store itself. No explanation, no follow-up.

When we returned to the store for clarification, we were told that the salesperson who handled our original order "wasn't knowledgeable" and had made several mistakes. That alone should have been a red flag, but we were then offered a new deal: a set of iPhone 15s with the assurance that they came with Apple Intelligence features. This was a major selling point for us, and we were told everything was ready once we ported our numbers over.

After receiving the phones, we discovered we needed to install a beta version of iOS to access these so-called "AI features." Even worse, we later found out that Apple Intelligence isn’t even supported on the iPhone 15—something any well-trained sales team should know. At that point, we had already completed the porting process and were fully committed to T-Mobile.

We contacted T-Mobile customer service and were eventually connected to a supervisor who apologized and promised to rectify the situation by shipping us iPhone 16s at no additional cost. It sounded like the right move—except that it never happened. No phones were ordered. No follow-up. Nothing but broken promises.

This experience has left me questioning T-Mobile’s training, integrity, and customer care at every level—from in-store staff to corporate oversight. At best, this was an example of gross miscommunication and incompetence. At worst, it feels like deliberate deception to lock us into a contract under false pretenses.

If you’re considering switching to T-Mobile, be very cautious. Ask for everything in writing, double-check every promise, and don’t assume you're getting the truth.

It’s been 10 years that I’m with them
I have good service everywhere I go

Unfortunately, I am writing this review to express my dissatisfaction with T-Mobile customer service. This week with T-mobile has been nothing near pleasant	to say the least. I went to the T-mobile main store in Pensacola on Davis hwy location, Sunday April 27th where multiple people helped with my claim on my phone. The employee made a warranty claim and put In for an overnight shipment after doing a eval on the issue, I was not provided a tracking number for this claim unable to login to my T-mobile account online with out a verification code I call the store on the update Monday April 28th, phone was not in and I was directed to call customer service for tracking, customer service was unrealistic as my phone is broken unable to get a verification code once again since it doesn’t work they could not help to inform me when my phone would be delivered to the store. I went into the store at 9 Mile Monday April 28th afternoon no shipment had been delivered for the phone. As now we are on Tuesday April 29th I made a call to the store and no phone had been delivered still, I went into the store that afternoon and I was told to come back around 6:30 because the phone was not processed therefore I did. The phone had now been processed at 6:30pm and was not able for me to receive because find my iPhone can not be turned off on my phone since it was unable to even turn on which every servicer at the Davis hwy store was aware of, so once again I am yet to get a phone that works. The kind lady that had patience with me at the 9 mile location as I was frustrated and not my best because I waited days now on this replacement phone that is now in store that I can not get started the process now for a insurance claim on the phone but its at this point after 7pm and that replacement phone is not scheduled to be delivered until Thursday May 1st. This was this most dysfunctional claim process I have experienced, the person that made the claim on April 27th at the DAVIS HWY location knew you could not access anything on my phone as it doesn’t turn on at all or anything processed a warranty claim in which before you sent the phone  back Find my iPhone has to be turned off which clearly couldn’t be done. I'll be without a phone for 5 days, 4 trips to in store to T-mobile, multiple phones call before I get a replacement phone, This is a nightmare and terrible customer service. Customers should not have to go through all this for a replacement phone but no your provided first to get a upgrade in store so you can have a phone same day then next option is doing a claim which is suppose to be overnight and ends up a disaster. In my case the wrong claim and still be without a phone after waiting. I will be contacting customer service on this issue, I will not be  paying for days of service for a phone that is not useable while waiting on a replacement for a mistake the store made, nor will I ever go through this process with T-Mobile or at least this store. Overall experience was stressful and not good business.

I was never given the promotion I was promised in the store and trying to undo this is a nightmare. I called TMobile because my bill seemed higher than it should.  That was 5 weeks ago.  Since then, I have spoken to three different agents.  Two promised to fix the problem and call me back within a specified time frame, but never did.  (Justin and Shydae).  Amelia is still trying to fix the problem, but in order to do so, she advised that my bill would be going UP because I had not been charged the correct rate plan.  Still no word on whether or not I will be paid back for the watch promotion I never got.  This is no way to do business and certainly no way to treat long-time T Mobile customers.  I would recommend you check your bill and be very careful when you go into a store and buy a product - they all speak very fast and clearly don't want to be pinned down on details so that they can OVERCHARGE you.  Enough!!

Update - Amelia informed me that I waited too long to catch their mistake and they cannot undo it, so I will not get the promotion I was entitled to.  This is nonsense.

Got say been really happy with Tmobile been with them  since  like 2008  right now I paying just $90 for 2 unlimited plans grandfather in at 2021 with price lock magenta 55 plus that truly unlimited not kidding I do 400 gigs  month in HD and 4k at 5G blazing speeds wife does 100 gigs both never throttled.  They gave me a S21 for free that I am using on Samsung  dex as a media server  to run both  85 inch 4k Tv and a 4k laser projector it's my internet I don't have another internet provider just S21 5G Dex and 50 gig hot spot. I have cheap phone I carry with me . The S21 sits on coffee table . I have not had issues service  has been great running at 270 Mps . My only complaint is don't trust the Tmobile store offers get what ever they offer in writing they tried to not give my wife her promised new phone and it took like 5 calls to customer service but they did send it. Got say price locked since 2021is a promise kept thanks Tmobile

I tried T-Mobile  two week free trial home internet. Nine days into the trial, I decided that it was not stable, dropped connections, slow speeds. I returned the equipment and was told that I owed nothing since I had returned the equipment prior to the two weeks. About three weeks later I received a bill for $65 from a collection agency representing T-Mobile. I called customer support and attempted to find out why I had received a bill. The person I spoke with had a very thick accent and it was difficult to understand what they were saying. I was told that I would have to go to the store were I had picked up the equipment and get a pin number???? I was forced to drive several miles to the store. Once there I explained to the rep and showed him the letter I had received. For 20 minutes he was on his tablet and phone. Finally he said he had taken care of the billing error, showed me that I had a $0.00 balance. I was satisfied until about a month later I received another bill from T-Mobile. I once again called and after half an hour spoke with a rep. This time the accent was not too bad, I could understand what he was saying. I explained to him my situation and he emailed me a pin which I read back to him. He put me on hold several times as he attempted to resolve my problem. Finally after a long period of time he came back on the line and said eveything was fixed. I am now happy, however that was not the end of this terrible experience with T-Mobile. about three weeks later yet another bill from T-Mobile. I decided to ignore this crap! Have heard nothing else. T-Molbile will never have my business and I warn anyone thinking of T-Mobile to think twice and find a company that does not try to rip you off!

They applied a wrong promotion code on my account. I was cheated. Their customer service cheated ( said contacted me and left a voice message but nothing actually happened). The bill is $800 more than what I should be paying. They agree but they said they can’t do anything. Ridiculous!

Horrible coverage. I bought the top of the line unlimited cell plan with a new S25. It took me about 15 minutes to get a signal in branson to write this review. I had way better coverage with prepaid ATT and an older LG velvet. I feel horribly ripped off

I would give zero stars if I could! Since signing up with T-Mobile I have had many issues but the biggest is being lied to and overcharged. When I signed up I was told at the store the two tablets and kids watch were free and $5 a month for service. I have been overcharged on every bill. They are charging $2.62 for each tablet and charging me $12 for the service on the watch. Initially customer service was helpful and would adjust my bill monthly. Then I was told that I would have to take the issue up with the store because they are the ones who made the mistake. I left multiple messages with the store and have yet to receive a call back. Customer service refuses to do anything else so I'm stuck paying an extra every month. On top of this their service sucks, they randomly make changes to your plan. I have spent many hours on the phone dealing with issues. Trust me it is not worth the savings. They are liars and thieves!!!!

T-Mobile is garbage. I let someone use my card 1 time to pay their bill. They did NOT save my card information for future billing, however, I was still charged for the full amount of their next bill. I called customer service, asked them to reverse the charge, and they said no, call the other bank to dispute the charge. It is a Friday, so I will still have to let the money be taken from my account so my financial institution can dispute the charge. This is taking money from my account that is needed for other bills through no fault of my own. I'm a Verizon customer, and they have NEVER charged the wrong card in over 7  years.

They are con artist save your money don’t listen to their time share style pitch went from paying 250 a month to 50 a month switching to a different carrier and that is with “trading in” a phone that was in perfect condition to get another one for “free” still had to pay it .

I experienced the worst customer service that I’ve ever had in my lifetime with this company. I called to check my devices to ensure they were all unlocked. The individual I talked to told me they were all unlocked at first, then said wait, there is one that he didn’t know whether it was or not. I asked him to unlock it. He said he couldn’t. I was confused and said I don’t understand why it can’t be cleared. All my devices were paid in full when they were purchased. He said what do you mean you don’t understand, I already told you. His reply was very rude. I replied no you didn’t. He didn’t explain anything. So he argued with me, talking over me. At this point I insisted that I wanted to talk to his supervisor. So I waited. He stated a couple of times that his supervisor was coming. I got tired of waiting. At this point I had it with this individual and told him that I had enough, that I was done and that I was going to get another provider. I did exactly that after being a customer with T Mobile for close to 10 years. I will not be talked to like that from a customer service rep and remain being a customer.

I've been a customer for nearly a decade. I had an old phone that was paid off and wasn't attached to a phone number that was supposed to be unlocked, but I would not. I called to have them unlock it, and they wouldn't because my bill was 1 day past due. 

The phone is mine. The account status has nothing to do with the device. I paid, and it was unlocked, but this is a ridiculous policy.
my friends are not getting my text messages spent 45 minutes on the phone to fix it work once when support was on the phone  now not working again ..but they there past due payment

We switched from US cellular to T Mobile two weeks ago and have had absolutely nothing but problems ever since! My daughter signed up for the upgrade to the I Phone 16 pro and was quoted that she would be given a $400 dollar credit for her current phone. We cleared her old phone and did a factory reset just as directed and sent it back to T Mobile as instructed. Shortly afterwards we were notified that our promotions were being voided because of “issues” with the phone that she had returned. They claimed that the screen was broken, that it was not the model that we had claimed and that the find my app was still active. As for the model claimed, all of this information was directly entered by their own sales representative. Concerning the find my app, all apps should have been cleared with the factory reset which we did exactly according to the T Mobile return instructions. And finally, although I am aware that cracked and broken screens are common, they are not for us! We have always used high quality screen protectors and cellphone cases to protect our cell phones! We have never had insurance plans on our phones and have never needed them! Every one of our phones have worked perfectly for four to five years. Right away I regretted not having taken a picture of the phone before we returned it! That is when I realized, why should I have to?!!!! I will never leave a cell phone service that I was content with for 17 years only to sign up for a service that you have to spend dozens of hours on the phone to resolve their issues and to have to be some paranoid detective to prove my own honesty while dealing with a deceitful and dishonest customer “service”! I have been with T Mobile for all of about two weeks and have zero love or faith in them at this point! Truthfully, the only reason that I gave them one star is because I don’t want to make it seem that I missed the star rating! Also, to be clear, the local sales representative that we dealt with was extremely nice and helpful and is even trying to work on this for us, but dealing with their customer “service” is nothing short of infuriating!

Like many long-term customers of T-mobile, I recently received notification of an increase of $5 per line going forward. Four days later, they had the audacity to send another notification claiming they are offering an additional line for "free".

Nothing about that offer is free. Clearly, our recent increases are being used to subsidize these "free lines" for everyone who chooses to activate them. 

Those of us without a use for the extra line should be able to opt-out. Our finances are being unfairly leveraged in order to support T-Mobile's scammy marketing tactics.

I was told the price increase was to cover increasing costs but somehow they have the spare finances to offer free lines? How can they increase rates followed by a promo for the same customers just four days later and not expect us to connect the dots?

Customer service didn't care at all and stood by the decision. I tried opting out but it was no use. They don't care about one customer being upset but I know a lot of people were affected by this and don't need the additional line. I would encourage everyone affected to contact them and let them know that what they are doing is unethical.

In downtown Chicago, my "ultra capacity 5G" can't even load Google Maps. Speed test? 0.2Mbps. Technician's genius solution: "Try standing near a window." For $85/month, I shouldn't need to hang out my apartment like a bat to get service!

Tmobile just raised the price for customers who been with them for long time. This is ridiculous. First of all they do not even have good service. They are losing their customer with this ridiculous price increase. I am leaving tmobile right now. They need to provide good service before they increase the price

Switched to T-Mobile for their "UnLIMiTeD dAtA" promo – BIG MISTAKE. The second I hit 50GB, my speeds dropped to dial-up levels. Customer service? A joke. Got some script-reading rep who told me "network congestion" is my problem. Oh, and that "free" phone they promised? Yeah, that magically became a $25/month charge.

tip: Screenshot EVERYTHING – their reps lie more than politicians.

Any time the weather is anything other than clear skies, the internet goes dead, the highest speed we get is 0.22 mps download and 2.20 upload, literally impossible to use. Their white box internet is even worse, we replaced it FIVE times because it would go into a boot loop, customer service told us just to keep replacing it. This went on for over a month and they still forced us to pay for wifi that we didn't even have. When we first got the white box, we bought it in a supposed bundle deal, which was all a complete lie. The salesman at the desk told us we would get a free phone and free smartwatch, in fact, we HAD to take the watch because it was with the bundle, if we didnt, we couldnt get the phone and wifi together. All lies. They ended up adding over $300 in charges to our monthly bill! When confronted, they told us that it was in fact a separate charge for the phone and watch, they refused to give us a refund. The customer service is absolutely horrible, as T-Mobile makes products with issues, then claims that we have 'an uncommon issue' and that they've never heard of it happening when in fact I can go just about anywhere on the internet and find complaints of boot-looping and scams. T-Mobile is absolutely a scam, DO NOT BUY THEIR PRODUCTS. They may be enticing with their prices, but you would be much better off with a slightly more expensive product that works, rather than one that only works sometimes.

If T-Mobile didn't have the 55+ plan we would've been canceling years ago after they merged with sprint. Sprint was so much better!

You WON’T even believe the kind of problems we have had with T-Mobile. Not counting the tech-support people who have no clue what they’re doing and horrible International coverage.

What is shocking and frightening is that two times they have messed up my 88 year-oldmother's eSIM so that she literally could not call out both times! But if there's an emergency? She's had two falls and a stroke already. Huge lawsuit potential for them you think they would get their act together but no the second time they completely DELETED HER eSIM when she went into the store because she was having some phone issues. WTF? How in the world do you even do that??!! 

So here I am overseas and I cannot reach my mother and no one can reach her, not even T-Mobile. I will be following a complaint with the FCC and the T-Mobile CEO's office. I should've done this before the last time. Read on….

So because of horrible service With T-Mobile, we went somewhere else for a while when they were combining with Sprint, but then came back because they had that 55+ plan which is a great deal. Is this so what you get what you pay for that's F the seniors? Because this is what happened….

When they were reporting my mother's number over, somehow they added some extra numbers or even one extra number I'm not sure and so when you called her, you were calling ENGLAND. No joke! You could hear the overseas ringtone and then a British woman on the end saying that the number was no good. Again how the F do you do that?? Complete incompetence!

And when I was having International problems paying for their international plan no service, traveling alone in a dangerous city, it took four phone calls before they could fix it. Plus I can't even tell you how many times I was on the phone trying to resolve my mother's issues not realizing that they had deleted her eSIM. 

It's like a circus of clowns there!

Mrs.Mona in technical support in T-Mobile customer service, a sweet and polite Asian lady. Best genuine customer service I have ever had in my life!!! I'm 40 yrs old. Can the CEO and top executives please times her yearly salary by 8 please!!! That genuine kindness and help can not be taught. That comes from the soul..🔥🔥🏆🏆🔥🔥

Ordered internet spoke to four different agents misleading misinformation runaround had a modem was not reaching entire house they told me to get a mashup had to exchange the modem first and get the whole unit The agent in the store told me I cannot get it unless I get more expensive plan to supervisor on the phone two days prior told me not no change just going to corporate store and exchange my equipment it is getting worse and worse day by day with this company would not recommend it to anybody but they don't care cuz they keep acquiring other companies you don't have many choices left anymore stay away if you can

I recently had the absolute pleasure of working with Regan from the Augusta T-Mobile call center, and I can confidently say she is the reason I’m now a loyal T-Mobile customer.

I was calling from overseas, trying to open a new account and activate an eSIM—a process that, according to another carrier (who I had visited in person, no less!), was “impossible.” But Regan? She made the impossible possible.

Despite the logistical challenges and the many hurdles involved, Regan patiently explored every solution to get me set up. Through it all, she remained upbeat, positive, and incredibly kind. Her tone was warm, courteous, and genuinely helpful—something that’s rare even in face-to-face service these days, let alone over the phone from thousands of miles away.

What truly stood out was her unwavering determination to help and the grace with which she handled every twist and turn. It’s this kind of dedication and empathy that not only solves problems but creates lasting customer loyalty.

I honestly expected a survey at the end of the call so I could sing her praises there—but when it didn’t come, I actually went out of my way to search for a way to share this review online. That’s how strongly I felt about the exceptional service I received.

Regan didn’t just set up my phone—she set the tone for what I believe will be a long and happy relationship between me and T-Mobile. Give this woman a raise, a promotion, and a parade—she deserves it!

Thank you, Regan. You’re the gold standard. 🙌📱💖

The list of things that went wrong are long, but in a nutshell, if you are considering signing up for T-Mobile on line or by phone, keep in mind the following:
1. They do not document anything that their customer service agents or the customer has access to, so if you get charged something different than what you expected, they can't prove who is right, so you lose.
2.  Chat and phone agents do not have access to any history of prior conversation, so if you have an issue that requires multiple communications, you have to start all over again every time.
3.  If you have a billing dispute, good luck.  Agents do not have access to billing history details, so expect nothing more from them to tell you what you owe.
4.  They lie.  Charged more than I signed up for.  Told me their would be no late fees or service interuption due to an unresolved billing dispute - wrong.  

In summary, if you feel the desire to move ahead with them, get everying in writing and in detail before signing up.  If you don't have it, you lose any and all disputes.

As soon as T-Mobile gets your money, customer service becomes non-existent. Most of their support is outsourced to Asia, and all you get is double-talk with no real solutions. It's frustrating, repetitive, and leads nowhere. The moment you need real help, you're left on your own.

T-Mobile might lure you in with competitive pricing, but don't be fooled — you get exactly what you pay for. If something goes wrong, be prepared for a long, exhausting ride with zero accountability.

This company has the worst customer service on Planet Earth. Rude. Unhelpful. Misinformed and they play games with your money. I'm disputing their fraudulent charges at my bank first thing in the morning. I would live with NO cellphone before l would go back to them.

After 12 years with T-Mobile, I’m done. In February 2025, I requested several backdated bill reprints, made it clear during two long calls on February 25 that I needed them by a specific deadline, and agreed to pay for the service. I was assured they’d be ordered and mailed promptly.

Five weeks passed yet nothing arrived. On April 7, I called again and spoke to a supervisor who said the reprints had been ordered but were mysteriously stuck in the mail room. He promised to investigate and follow up. He called back on April 11, but left no message when I couldn’t answer.

On April 16, I called again and got a different supervisor who said everything I’d previously been told was incorrect, and that there was a completely different reason for the delay. To top it off, that agent ended the call by disconnecting on me and didn’t call back.

This runaround went on for seven weeks, during which I received zero follow through and completely contradictory information. These bill reprints were urgently needed for litigation, and T-Mobile couldn’t manage a simple request despite multiple opportunities.

I’ll be closing all my accounts—three phone lines and a watch. Save yourself the frustration. Take your business elsewhere.
I do not recommend this company. If you are not careful, they can charge you extra fees and you may not even realize it.

I've been a loyal MetroPCS (now T-Mobile) customer for nearly 30 years. Recently, I went to a branch to get a new phone. The price on the shelf said $245 — but I was told that’s only for new customers. As an existing customer, I'd have to pay $400 for the same phone. Then the rep offered a "deal": if I buy two phones, it would be $300 total, because I have a family plan. What kind of logic is that?

Naturally, I walked out and bought the same phone unlocked, already T-Mobile network ready, from somewhere else.

All good, right? Just pop in my SIM and go. Nope.

Back at the MetroPCS store, they told me they had to do it, even though it was my same SIM card, same phone number, same everything — and they charged me $25 just to activate my own phone.

Loyalty is a two-way street. After decades as a customer, I expected fair treatment — not upsells, hidden fees, and price games. I stayed because it used to be a win-win. Now? with T-Mbile, It just feels like a rip-off. I'm looking forward to move to another Company and never come back.

The customer service was good, but the time it takes to purchase a new phone, and new line and get back out the door is far too long. Im a single mom without any help, and was there with my youngest who is 2 and autistic. The process is just much too long, the young lady that was helping me was also helping other people in between finalizing my purchases. Kind of annoying when youre sitting there with a crying baby and she keeps walking away to help someome else,who came in AFTER we did. Like i said, the gal was good but there were 4 other employees there as well, some just restocking.

I was a T-Mobile customer for 7 years.The store on 96th and broadway has the very worst customer service. They are so rude when you ask a question or try to get help the people on the floor get so annoyed because you're either interrupting their social interactions with their friends or their screen time.
I then decided it was time to leave T mobile, come to find out there's still charging me for my phone and insurance which I no longer had. The guy on the phone was nice but it goes I hear you I hear you I'm like no you don't and he wasted my time. This is a shame cuz I used to be very good but that note is no longer the case and don't go but don't go to AT&T either
Not sure why the rating is so low. They have done right by me since the beginning.
Do NOT switch to T-Mobile. When I switched, I was told that I would receive a new iPhone, this seasons baseball package, HULU for free and Appletv free for 6 months.  I received NONE of these.

Trying to cancel T-Mobile is an awful experience. When my wife and I signed up, they made us add a third line to our account because you apparently can’t have just two. We never got a phone for this line.

Fast forward several years and we port our numbers over to another provider so that we wouldn’t lose them. We then call T-Mobile and they will not talk to us because the account isn’t ‘verified’ regardless of us giving them the PIN number on our account. They keep wanting to text us a verification link to that number that never had a phone. 

Finally they have a verification team member call us and I have to take a freaking selfie with a picture of my drivers license to send to them. They then said they canceled my account and I verified with them numerous times that all lines were canceled which they confirmed. Now today we get a new full month charge withdrawn from our bank account. We call back and THEY WILL NOT TALK YO ME WITHOUT TEXTING ME AT THAT NUMBER THST DOESNT EXIST!! Now they want to do the whole drivers license/selfie thing again which they say takes an hour for them to call back but last time it was the next day. 

Our experience with T-Mobile is horrible and we would not recommend their service. If you are a couple that needs just two lines, be aware that that third mandatory line will screw you over

Been with the company for 11 years. They only care about money. Charged over $200 for a restoration fee that was already paid the same day—for three lines. I don’t know about the West Coast, but on the East Coast, their service is terrible unless you’re in a center city.
BEWARE. Other companies offer much better service coverage with much better pricing.
the worst company i have ever been a customer of.  they do not seem to be concerned about feed back from their customers.  ever time i have a problem on their side it is a two day waste of my time.

I went in to Baxter Mn store to switch from ATT . We talked to the store”manager” who wrote down on paper what the cost and plan we would get. He told us to pay off the last 2 months of my wife’s phone .I did and brought in the receipt. He said he would pay that off. He grabbed a phone scleped a screen save and gave us no paper work. A month later the bill comes, and it was double. He added -with no approval or discussion 3 trackers , insurance , charged $40 for the screen saver he put on and $20 for a charge cube we did not need. He did not apple for T-Mobile to pay the $150 pay on ATT phone as promised. Call customer service and they refunded some of the BS but not all. They got a document of charges that we never got or saw. This was the worst retail experience I have ever had.

I tried often on for three days to pay my T-Mobile bill. The mobile app didn’t work always reject my password. 611 didn’t work. It would cut in and out couldn’t complete nothing I have a 55+ plan so I want. To a T-Mobile store to pay my bill because it was due 4/9/25 By the time I got out I had paid $65. They charge me five dollars to pay my bill in the store because there. System doesn’t work by design shame on you T-Mobile rip off crooked, dishonest

edits.text: in the past month, I have spent $18 twice for charging cables at T-Mobile. Neither one of them would last two weeks they are made in China very low quality. This is what T-Mobile thinks of their customers.

text:
Excellent downtown Long Beach T-Mobile store! 
I saw a busy Saturday store's staff handled the customers like pro's.
I brought in an old S6 edge with all kinds of issues with secured data, and all, and they did a complete transfer to my S25. It wasn't easy!
A special shout out to David Hernandez and Saul Hernandez, Thank you for the Top-end customer service.

T-Mobile is a Scam. I was with them for 3 years, and then when I wanted to cancel my service with them, they said they couldn't cancel me today, but had to wait 3 weeks until the billing cycle ended to cancel me. I was upset about it, and let them know that they are just scamming people to get extra days out of them. In the age of Artificial Intelligence, and lightning fast internet, they have to wait 3 weeks to cancel me. Do yourself a favor, and don't open an account with them.

We switched to TMobile from paying for 3 other separate services, 2 phones and internet, thinking we would save money with one service. So far, I've been billed 3 times from March 3/4th until April 8th. The bill for this month is substantially higher than what we were told it would be. Plus, I have trouble getting in to YouTube. I'm switching back. At least I know what I'll be paying. Not a good plan for seniors!

text: The 24 month bill credit is a scam.  The screen was not cracked and phone worked perfectly,  In fact it worked better than the S25 Ultra I traded it for.  They say you have 30 days to return the trade in.  Which I did.  They said my phone was cracked.  That is a lie.  They now say I can't return the S25 because I only had 16 days to  return that (yet I have 30 days to return the other).  It is a scam.  They never give credit like they say.  I have been with them over 10 years and have never once received the promised credit.  STAY AWAY from T MOBILE

They are the worst! I know my six digit pin and it never works with T-Mobile and they’re telling me I need to go to the store and reset it. Their new app is terrible and I’ve been calling them telling them. I am not able to see my usage and they don’t care. Half the time when I try to dial out, the phone won’t even ring to the person I’m trying to call! The worst company I’ve ever dealt with. I’m going to Verizon.

their customer support is shit. i bought a new screen protector for my ultra s23 when i bought the phone brand new. 6-28-23. the deal was i buy a screen protector now, i get the rest free when needed. I called the 611 like the guy said to in the store because the T-Mobile store no longer have them in-stores well i need one and they are telling me i have to pay again and then they will reimburse me. noooooo i already paid. so now i am getting nothing but the run around. and still no screen protector and i also have insurance on my phone and still getting a run around. my experience with this company and been nothing more than a pain in the ass to deal with. and nobody knows a damn thing about anything. SO HOW MANY OTHERS ARE THEY BENDING OVER.....

I was charged for WiFi calls that are supposed to be free when made from outside the country to the U.S. — a clear billing error. I contacted T-Mobile customer service and spoke with two supervisors. One of them didn’t even know that these types of WiFi calls are free, which was very concerning. The second supervisor acknowledged the mistake and promised the charges would be waived at the store.

When I went to the store, the staff was unhelpful. They claimed there was no note from the supervisor and said they couldn’t access any of the communication I had with customer service. I was left without a solution.

In the end, I had to pay the charges just to avoid two late fees that had already been added to my bill. It’s frustrating to be misled, passed around, and then penalized financially for their own error.

I don’t plan to have service with them again. This company has acted dishonestly — nothing but a bunch of thieves.

What is wrong with the USPS in Gainesville florida

 The store can't take my payment to pay my account  and only give me the number to t mobile corporate and that's useless so I'm closing my account I would recommend you stay away from this company like the plague 
 They are not helpful or corporate with the customer  they want me to come into the store and I won't do it again 
 They are absolutely the worst company to do business with not helpful in any way
I would love to give them a big fat 0

The absolute worst customer service experience I have ever had.. I spent my entire day off trying to cancel an account and still got no resolution. I was lied to several times, “I will transfer you to my supervisor” just to be hung up on, “I will have someone call you back within 60-90 minutes” several times and received absolutely no calls back “ You need to go to the store to get that done” and when I arrive at the store, “No, we cannot help you, you have to call our customer service”. .. what a nightmare. Completely ridiculous. And as I am writing this now, it is still not fixed. I would recommend staying far away from this company. I think this is the only bad review I have ever written, but my experience was so bad, I have to warn people.

Illegally billing and charging an account that has been closed. Put me on autopay on a credit card without my consent. They've done this to 10s of thousands of past customers. Their customer service reps lie, and don't listen to the customer. I have it on recorded calls. This company is fraudulent and their service doesn't work anymore. Their lines are down 50% of the time lately. Stay away. Buyer Beware. I've reported them to the FTC and the local State's Attorneys Office and I wouldn't be shocked if a class action lawsuit was filed soon.

Switched away from T-Mobile to a different carrier. Specifically asked if everything was closed with the account or if there was anything else I needed to do to close our relationship. The agent who I spoke with said no, I had done all that was required. They still billed me on autopay for 2 months before I caught it. I called and they said they had no record of me requesting to cancel our 3rd line, so they’re saying charges were vaild. Horrible service and experience.
T mobile keep charging me bill,when I cancel the service last month 14 march,they still send me bill,which one not fear,T mobile rip me off,they are lier,I don't know who can help,me.

I signed up for a 55+ account. It is supposed to be $55+tax for two phone lines.
I've had the account 2 days. They post a bill due later in the month for $111. 
I called customer service. They are now saying they can't look at the details of the account and I have to go to a store and show an ID. It's a security thing.
That is BS. If it was for security, then they wouldn't
 be able to transfer my number to their service. It's only because I have an issue with their billing that they are making me jump through hoops.
Apparently they have institutionalized this. Ive spoken with 3 different individuals and they all said the same thing.
Additionally, there was a "deal" on their version of air tags. $1 per month each. I ordered 2.
3 were sent to my house. The the total charge for each is $3.40.

Follow up. I went to the local store. The guy there was helpful. He had no idea why the phone folks had so much trouble.
The issue with the costs was due to them setting up the 2 lines separately instead of as a bundle. Also it was a digital only offer which was a bit cheaper.

The extra phone tag was just a mistake.
Terrible signal, slow data and random fees. Customer servise is no help. Not worth the hassel.

I have recently found myself in a domestic assault by my partner of 10 yrs. And had to get a restraining order against him. He is the primary account holder for our tmobile account that renders him in control of my phone line, and the protective order specifically states he is violating the court order if he changes anything on my phone service or utilities. So ofcoarse he suspends my phone line on the account that we have financed and are still paying for 2 devices and he refuses to release my number I have had for 19yrs.  I co tact tmobile to ask what can be done so I can keep my number and separate our accounts. They tell me to fill out a form to their safe connection act office and submit photo documentation of my protective order via the link at the end of the form. So I try and they try and try again and the link will not work. At best I was g8ven a printed form to mail into their safe connection act office in NM. With a certified copy of my protective order and I did. It took a week to get there and I read on this form that I wouldn't incur fees or charges to separate my line and start my own account. With tmobile. So they call me a week later and I missed the call and I got the voicemail from the president's secretary, to call her back on the number she left, I called and called and called. No answer no call back. So I'm calling customer care and chatting with customer care and going to tmobile stores to get this taken care of and finally some one helps me with setting up my account and they inform me that I will be charged a 250.00 deposit to establish a line of my own after running my credit report. Without even looking at the fact that it's been my number and my card with my name on it making the payments on the account since we opened it last year.  I said but it states I will not be charged to separate my line from a primary account and set up my own account. So she suspends the line again pending a resolution for this 250.00 deposit that they want to charge me for starting my own account.  So I'm calling and I'm emailing and at one point I was even told to send in another form and copy of my documents thru the mail to get them to call me back again. What?! Are you kidding me? And wait another 7 days without a phone? Nobody can help me with this issue. I feel like I'm being victimized all over again with ever call comes a new agent and I have to go over everything all over again and again!!! This makes no sense. If they are relieving me of the balance on my device and charging the primary for the device why would they charge me a deposit to set up my account? I didn't ask to be in this situation and it's the first time I've ever been in this situation.  Along with all the costs of relocating, and starting over how am I supposed to come up with this deposit that I wasn't expecting to get. ? 
This is horrible. Nobody knows how to fix this issue and I keep getting the go round a gain and a gain. And again

T-mobile as previously stated is no better and certainly no worse than all the other overgrown telecommunication services.
It would be nice when awaiting service in one of their many stores that they adopted the system used in restaurants or other service orientated businesses to offer a locator/beeper. Instead of sitting around for an hour plus with an indifferent service attitude! One could continue shopping or whstever until recalled.
